items.hook: Happy to help you narrow this down.
items.bridge: For a salon booking, the service direction, time window, and rough current hair state matter more than a quick yes or no.
items.next_step: Ask for service direction, preferred time, and rough current hair state.
items.handoff_suggestion: Route to Catcher if the user replies with booking facts.

items.hook: That is totally normal for a first visit.
items.bridge: A lot of first-time salon clients do not need more options, they need a simple way to narrow what fits their life and schedule.
items.next_step: Acknowledge the uncertainty and ask whether the user wants something easier to manage, a color refresh, or a bigger change plus the time window.
items.handoff_suggestion: Route to Catcher if the user shares direction and timing.

items.hook: We can keep this simple and work from your event timing first.
items.bridge: When an event is close, the date, slot, and whether this is a cut, color, or styling need matter more than a long style lecture.
items.next_step: Ask when the event is, when the user can come in, and what service direction they need.
items.handoff_suggestion: Route to Catcher once the user shares timing facts.

items.hook: I can help make the price answer more useful.
items.bridge: For hair services, the real price often changes with cuts versus color, length, bleach, and add-ons, so one quick scope question helps a lot.
items.next_step: Ask whether this is a cut, color, perm, or maintenance visit.
items.handoff_suggestion: Route to Catcher if the user shares scope.

items.hook: Nice to see you back here.
items.bridge: For returning salon clients, it helps to start from what they want to refresh, change, or maintain this time instead of restarting from zero.
items.next_step: Ask what they liked last time and what they want to change this time, plus when they hope to come in.
items.handoff_suggestion: Route to FollowUp or Catcher depending on the user's reply.

items.hook: We can keep this easy and low-pressure.
items.bridge: Some hair concerns feel more personal than they look in a comment, so the goal is to stay helpful without making the user feel exposed.
items.next_step: Reply with empathy and lightly invite the user to share service direction and timing.
items.handoff_suggestion: Route to Catcher if the user continues with details.
